grounding_note: Libel/Slander/Defamation requires a damaging FALSE statement; Online Harassment/Doxing/Bullying covers campaigns of harmful online conduct regardless of falsity. Row 344 mixed defamation into an employment-harassment complaint. What was actually said/posted — false factual claims versus threats/private-info exposure — is the fact users summarize away as 'attacking me online.'

scenarios.opening_query: Someone is attacking me online relentlessly and it's affecting my business and my sleep.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The posts state flatly that I stole client deposits and lost my license — both provably false, and two clients canceled citing the posts.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The posts claim I stole client deposits and lost my license — both provably false, and two clients canceled over it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the content makes false factual claims causing reputational harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The posts aren't factual claims — it's daily insults, my home address published, and 'we know where you sleep' messages.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's not claims about facts — it's daily insults, my home address posted, and 'we know where you sleep' messages.
scenarios.vetting_note: False-statement vs threat/doxing content is the doctrinal line; identical opening supports both.

scenarios.opening_query: A former friend is spreading lies about me everywhere and I want it stopped legally.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's gone past 'lies' — she posted my new address and workplace to a group that's been showing up, and shares screenshots of my private medical records.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's beyond lies — she posted my address and workplace to a group that shows up in person, plus screenshots of my medical records.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the conduct is doxing/coordinated harassment beyond false statements
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's specifically the false story that I embezzled from our old employer — she repeats it to anyone who'll listen and I lost a job offer over it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the false embezzlement story — she tells everyone, and it cost me a job offer.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Spreading lies' can mask doxing; the conduct inventory is the disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: A review posted about my restaurant is destroying us and it was written by our competitor.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's part of a whole campaign — fake reviews, poached staff under a broken non-compete, and copied menus; we want one business case against them, not just the review.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The review is one piece — fake reviews, staff poached against a non-compete, copied menus. We want a full business case against them.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the review is one tort or part of a business-versus-business dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just the one review — but it falsely claims a health-department shutdown that never happened.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's just the review — but it invents a health-department shutdown that never happened.
scenarios.vetting_note: Scope fact: single false statement vs unfair-competition campaign selects tort vs business litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: My teenager is being tormented by classmates on social media and the school shrugs it off.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || Administrative Law > Student Rights
scenarios.hidden_fact: The school's own accounts are involved — staff let students run the 'official' class page where the worst posts live, and the district refuses to apply its anti-bullying policy. We want the district held to account.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's on the school's own class page that staff supervise — and the district won't apply its own anti-bullying policy. We want the district accountable.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the school district's own conduct/policy failure is the target
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The school isn't really involved — it's a private group chat run by two specific kids, and we want civil action against their families.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: School's not involved — it's a private group chat run by two kids, and we want to act against their families.
scenarios.vetting_note: Defendant identity (district vs families) reroutes between civil harassment and education law.

scenarios.opening_query: An anonymous account keeps posting about me and I feel watched everywhere I go.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: The posts include photos of me taken that same day — my gym, my car, my office lobby. Whoever it is physically follows me, and I want a protective order.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The posts have same-day photos of me — gym, car, office lobby. They're physically following me. I want a protective order.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether physical following accompanies the online conduct
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's all online — recycled old photos and rumors; nobody has followed me in person.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's purely online — old photos and rumors. Nobody's followed me physically.
scenarios.vetting_note: Physical-surveillance evidence converts an online tort into protective-order territory.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex posted things about me that are ruining my reputation with everyone we know.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying
scenarios.hidden_fact: The posts come with direct threats — 'you'll regret leaving' with photos of my new apartment building. We lived together three years; I want protection first, reputation second.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They come with threats — 'you'll regret leaving' over photos of my new building. We lived together three years. Protection comes first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether threats from a former household member make protection the primary need
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No threats — it's a fabricated story about me abusing him, posted where my family and boss saw it, and it's costing me real relationships.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No threats — it's a fabricated abuse story posted where my family and boss saw it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Threat content plus FAPA relationship elevates safety over reputation; both variants are common real intakes.

scenarios.opening_query: A coworker told our whole office something about me that torpedoed my promotion.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Libel/Slander/Defamation || Labor & Employment > Discrimination - Employee
scenarios.hidden_fact: What she 'told' them was that I'm gay — which is true and was private — and management pulled the promotion right after. This is workplace discrimination, not a lie problem.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She outed me — it's true, but it was private — and management pulled my promotion right after. That's discrimination, not defamation.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the statement was false (defamation) or a true disclosure followed by discriminatory action
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She invented an affair between me and our director — completely false — and HR treated it as fact when passing me over.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She invented an affair with our director — totally false — and HR treated it as true when passing me over.
scenarios.vetting_note: Truth defeats defamation; the truth/falsity fact plus employer reaction selects the employment route.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone hacked into my accounts and now embarrassing things about me are circulating online.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Online Harrassment/Doxing/Bullying || Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law || Consumer Law > Identity Theft
scenarios.hidden_fact: The bigger problem is the intrusion itself — they're still inside my email and cloud storage, accessed my business banking, and I need the unauthorized-access side handled.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The real problem is the intrusion — they're still in my email and cloud storage and reached my business banking. I need the unauthorized-access side handled.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether ongoing unauthorized system access is the operative harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The account access was closed quickly — what continues is an ex-friend's circulation campaign of what was taken, and I want that pursued.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The access is closed — what continues is my ex-friend circulating what was taken. That's what I want pursued.
scenarios.vetting_note: Intrusion-vs-distribution: the taxonomy's computer-law label names unauthorized access explicitly.
